title: I Worked For Chaff, And Earning Wheat
content:
I worked for chaff, and earning wheat
    Was haughty and betrayed.
    What right had fields to arbitrate
    In matters ratified?

    I tasted wheat, -- and hated chaff,
    And thanked the ample friend;
    Wisdom is more becoming viewed
    At distance than at hand.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "I Worked For Chaff, And Earning Wheat", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
